arts.ttl: Champions League | Feynoord v AC Milan: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: Feyenoord will face AC Milan in the Champions League knockout playoff round with a series of key players sidelined. Manager Brian Priske has been sacked, leaving the Dutch side in turmoil ahead of a huge match in prospect. Austrian defender Gernot Trauner is unlikely to feature for a third consecutive match. The absence list also includes goalkeeper Justin Bijlow, as well as Calvin Stengs, Chris-Kevin Nadje, and Ramiz Zerrouki. Timon Wellenreuther is expected to continue in goal for the Dutch side. The post Champions League | Feynoord v AC Milan: Probable starting lineups appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: AC Milan XI vs Feyenoord – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: AC Milan head to Feyenoord this evening in the first leg of their Champions League play-off tie. A disappointing defeat at Dinamo Zagreb on matchday eight cost the Rossoneri an automatic place in the Round of 16 and the Dutch side now await. arts.ttl: “Champions in My Squad”: Conceicao Prepares Milan for Feyenoord Clash
arts.sum: AC Milan manager Sergio Conceicao recently addressed the media, laying out his thoughts ahead of the crucial Champions League playoff first-leg match against Feyenoord. With the highly anticipated game in Rotterdam looming, Conceicao conveyed his strategies, player expectations, and the overall team philosophy. “There are more than four champions in my squad, not just the ones up front. These four are part of the solutions we have available. It’s not like we have many options. We have 15 outfield players plus some youngsters.
